what_it_does: Defines a 24 by 24 SVG outline icon of a folder using a single path with no fill and currentColor stroke.
observations: The SVG uses viewBox="0 0 24 24" with explicit width and height of 24.
Rendering is stroke-based with fill="none" and stroke="currentColor".
Stroke styling sets width 1.5 with round line caps and joins.
The folder shape is encoded as one path element, including the tab and outer container outline.
standouts: Color inherits from surrounding text or CSS via currentColor.
Compact inline SVG suitable for icon systems.
conventions: Uses stroke-only icon styling with rounded caps and joins.
Keeps geometry inline rather than referencing external symbols or definitions.
notes: This file is a minimal currentColor SVG folder icon asset.
questions: Is the 1.5 stroke width the intended standard for the surrounding icon set?
purpose_vs_actual: The path/name suggests a folder icon, and the SVG content matches by drawing a folder outline.
intention_signals: It appears intended to provide a reusable folder icon that can inherit color from UI context while maintaining a consistent 24px outline style.
contribution: Contributes a portable, themeable folder glyph with simple stroke geometry that a fused system would keep as an asset for folder, directory, or file-navigation affordances.
summary_for_embedding: A 24px inline SVG asset drawing a currentColor stroke-only folder outline with rounded stroke styling.
intention_for_embedding: This was for representing folders or directories in a UI icon set while inheriting visual color from context.
